controls.title: Limit system access to authorized users

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Limit information system access to authorized users, processes acting on behalf of authorized users, and devices (including other information systems).

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Access control policies (e.g., identity- or role-based policies, control matrices, and cryptography) control access between active entities or subjects and passive entities or objects in information systems.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Authorized users of the information system are identified.

controls.title: Limit system access to the types of transactions

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Limit information system access to the types of transactions and functions that authorized users are permitted to execute.

controls.title: Create and retain system audit logs

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Create and retain system audit logs and records to the extent needed to enable the monitoring, analysis, investigation, and reporting of unlawful or unauthorized system activity.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: An event is any observable occurrence in an organizational system. Audit records can be generated at various levels of abstraction.
